qualifying_org_definition: Public or private non-profit agency (IDAPA 16.03.04.251.01.c lists 'Unpaid work, with a public or private non-profit agency' as a standalone qualifying ABAWD activity). No Idaho rule imposes an in-state location, registration, MOU, or county-approved-site requirement on the nonprofit hosting general volunteer hours.
qualifyingOrgShort: Any public or private non-profit agency for which the recipient performs unpaid work; no in-state, registration, or county-approved-site requirement found in Idaho rules.
verification_instrument: No Idaho-specific volunteer-hours form identified in regulation or public DHW guidance. IDAPA 16.03.04.252 (Proof Required for ABAWDs) is minimal and names no form for documenting volunteer hours; the DHW Employment & Training page names no form either. Federal default applies: caseworker verifies compliance at certification/recertification and via change reporting, using case notes, pay stubs, or a letter from the nonprofit confirming hours. Self-attestation acceptable absent countervailing evidence.
reportingCadence: Ongoing compliance is verified at certification/recertification (typically every 12 months). Change reporting is triggered when total work or work program hours drop below 80/month: ABAWDs must report within the first 10 days of the following month (IDAPA 16.03.04.254).
accepts_outofstate_detail: IDAPA 16.03.04.251.01.c says 'Unpaid work, with a public or private non-profit agency' with no geographic restriction on the nonprofit's location. No Idaho rule or guidance requires the nonprofit to be Idaho-based or registered. Absent an explicit restriction, federal default applies (any qualifying nonprofit), but the state is formally silent.
hours_formula_detail: General unpaid-volunteer lane = flat 80 hrs/month (about 20 hrs/week). IDAPA 16.03.04.251.01 states verbatim 'The person must work at least eighty (80) hours per month,' and subsection .01.c lists 'Unpaid work, with a public or private non-profit agency' as a standalone qualifying activity. The regulation text contains NO benefit-allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage formula. Idaho separately offers a 'public workfare' option (assigned hours at a workfare site); that distinct workfare lane — not general nonprofit volunteering — is where any allotment/minimum-wage assigned-hours calculation would live. The general volunteer path is a flat 80 hours and does not scale with allotment or wage.
formulaDetail: N/A for the general volunteer lane — flat 80 hrs/month. The allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage assigned-hours concept applies only to Idaho's separate optional 'public workfare' program (assigned hours at a workfare site), not to general unpaid nonprofit volunteering. Idaho's 2026 minimum wage is the federal $7.25/hr (Idaho Code 44-1502 ties the state rate to the federal rate; local minimum wages are statutorily prohibited), so even in the workfare lane there is no sub-state wage variation.
geographic_waivers: None for FY2026. Idaho has no statewide ABAWD time-limit waiver and enforces ABAWD statewide (long-standing no-waiver state); no FNS FY2025-2029 ABAWD county waiver for Idaho was found. IDAPA 16.03.04.251.05 references a discretionary high-unemployment / lack-of-jobs county mechanism ('ABAWDs residing in a county identified by the Department as having high unemployment or lack of jobs may not be subject to the three (3) month limitation'), but no specific counties are currently designated as waived in public guidance, and OBBBA/HR1 (2025) narrowed standard waiver authority (now limited to areas above 10% unemployment or noncontiguous AK/HI).
geographicWaivers: None in FY2026. Idaho has no statewide ABAWD waiver and enforces statewide; no FNS FY2025-2029 Idaho ABAWD county waiver found. IDAPA 251.05 references a high-unemployment/lack-of-jobs county exemption mechanism but no counties are currently designated in public guidance. OBBBA (2025) narrowed standard waiver authority to >10% unemployment areas or noncontiguous AK/HI.
submissionMechanism: No dedicated online portal or named form found for volunteer-hour reporting. Compliance is documented at certification/recertification and via change reporting; the implied channel for the proof (e.g., a nonprofit letter or case-note verification) is the county DHW office (in person, by mail, or via DHW contact). Idaho self-service is via the idalink / iSelfServe system and the DHW call center (877-456-1233) for change reporting, but no ABAWD-volunteer-specific submission tool is published.
submission_deadline: Compliance is verified at certification/recertification (typically every 12 months). Change-reporting deadline: within the first 10 days of the month following any month in which total work or work program hours drop below 80 (IDAPA 16.03.04.254).
submissionDeadline: Verified at certification/recertification (typically every 12 months); change reporting due within the first 10 days of the month following any month hours drop below 80 (IDAPA 16.03.04.254).
notes:
FY2026 VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22): Classification = federal_80 confirmed against primary sources retrieved this pass. (1) IDAPA 16.03.04.251.01 (LII): verbatim 'The person must work at least eighty (80) hours per month.' Subsection .01.c lists 'Unpaid work, with a public or private non-profit agency' as a standalone qualifying ABAWD activity. The regulation text contains NO benefit-allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage formula and (in the LII extract) no workfare language inside 251 itself. (2) Multiple FNA/DHW/Ballotpedia/Idaho Capital Sun sources confirm the ways to comply: work for pay/in-kind/unpaid/volunteer 80 hrs/mo; participate in a work (E&T) program 80 hrs/mo; a combination totaling 80; OR 'participate in public workfare for assigned number of hours a month.' Public workfare is enumerated as a SEPARATE, distinct option — so the general nonprofit volunteer lane is the flat 80-hour path, and any assigned-hours/allotment math applies only to the separate workfare lane. This is the decisive federal_80 finding. (3) IDAPA 16.03.04.254 (LII): verbatim 'ABAWDs must report within the first ten (10) days of the month following the date of change if total work or work program hours drop below eighty (80) hours per month.' (4) No Idaho-specific volunteer-hours verification form found in IDAPA 252 or on the DHW Employment & Training page; verificationFormName set to 'unknown' (downgraded from the prior file's federal-default prose so the calculator does not display a non-existent named form). (5) ENFORCEMENT/WAIVERS: Idaho has no statewide ABAWD waiver and enforces statewide (consistently reported as a no-waiver state); no FY2025-2029 FNS Idaho ABAWD county waiver found. IDAPA 251.05 high-unemployment county mechanism exists but no counties are currently designated. OBBBA/HR1 (2025) expanded the ABAWD age ceiling to 64 and narrowed waiver authority. (6) MINIMUM WAGE: Idaho 2026 = federal $7.25 (Idaho Code 44-1502 ties the state rate to federal; local minimum wages prohibited). No regional/sub-state wages. Because classification is federal_80, minimumWageUsd = 0 and regionalWages = []. (7) REMOTE STANCE: silent — no Idaho source addresses remote/virtual volunteering (prior file said 'unknown'; normalized to 'silent'). (8) COUNTY VARIATION: false — Idaho SNAP is state-administered (single statewide rule set and eligibility system); no material county-level variation in the volunteer lane.

PRIOR-FILE CONTEXT (retained): Earlier passes extracted the full IDAPA 16.03.04 rules PDF (adminrules.idaho.gov / state blob storage, updated through 7-1-24) and confirmed 251.01.c is a standalone volunteer pathway with no prerequisite enrollment; Section 252 names no Idaho volunteer form; JSAP/E&T (MAXIMUS-operated) is a parallel qualifying track, not a mandatory prestep before volunteer hours count; no pre-approved volunteer site list, MOU, or state provider registration for general-volunteer nonprofits was found (the DHW 'PDAP MOU Template' applies only to E&T community partners seeking funding). requiredPrestep = none.

UNCERTAINTY (honest): The exact instrument a caseworker uses to accept volunteer hours at recertification is not published by Idaho; we infer the federal default (nonprofit letter / case-note verification / self-attestation). The form_required_fields list reflects a generic federal-baseline nonprofit verification letter, NOT a named Idaho form. submissionMechanism (paper/county office) is inferred from the absence of any published ABAWD-volunteer-specific online tool; idalink/iSelfServe and the DHW call center handle general change reporting.
conflictsWithPrior: verification_instrument / verificationFormName: prior file described a federal-default verification instrument; this pass sets verificationFormName explicitly to 'unknown' because no Idaho-specific volunteer-hours form exists in any primary source, so the public calculator must not imply a named form.
remote_stance: prior file 'unknown' -> normalized to 'silent' (no Idaho source addresses remote/virtual volunteering and none prohibits it).

geographic_waivers wording: prior conclusion (no FY2026 Idaho waiver; enforced statewide) retained; added OBBBA/HR1 (2025) context — age ceiling raised to 64 and standard waiver authority narrowed to >10% unemployment / noncontiguous AK/HI.

Added explicit classification ('federal_80'), minimumWageUsd (0), and regionalWages ([]) fields and the federal_80-vs-public-workfare distinction (workfare is a separate enumerated option, NOT the general volunteer lane) for the public calculator; no substantive change to the underlying hours conclusion (flat 80/month).
unresolved: No Idaho-specific volunteer-hours verification form exists in public rules (IDAPA 252) or DHW guidance; the exact document a caseworker accepts at recertification is unknown (verificationFormName = 'unknown'). The form_required_fields are a generic federal-baseline nonprofit letter, not a named Idaho form.
Remote/virtual volunteering eligibility is not addressed in any Idaho primary source — treated as 'silent'.

Out-of-state 501(c)(3) acceptance is not explicitly stated; inferred 'silent/likely-allowed' because IDAPA 251.01.c imposes no in-state or registration requirement, but no source affirmatively confirms cross-state providers are accepted.

Idaho DHW Employment & Training page and the Idaho Capital Sun 2026 article returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch this pass; their content was corroborated via WebSearch snippets and Ballotpedia rather than a directly fetched quoted paragraph.

submissionMechanism (paper/county office) is inferred from the absence of a published ABAWD-volunteer-specific online submission tool; no source affirmatively names the channel for submitting volunteer-hour proof.
